topic.what: Cyclic equalizability of two words over arbitrary finite alphabet
topic.how: Proved equivalence to Parikh vector equality through constructive simultaneous insertion algorithm using block-group partition and reading with coprime step size
topic.why_matters: Completely resolves open problem from Shinagawa-Nuida 2025; enables analysis of card-based protocols under random cuts; establishes bridge between Abelian and cyclic equivalence

characteristics.evidence: Section 5.1: m = number of cycles in permutation
characteristics.context: n = word length, m = cycle count

novelty: Complete characterization of cyclic equalizability for arbitrary finite alphabet
Proof that cyclic equalizability equals Parikh vector equality
Constructive algorithm transforming Abelian-equivalent words to cyclically equivalent via simultaneous insertion
Generalization of binary result to arbitrary alphabet size
open_problems: Characterize cyclic equalizability for k>=3 words
Analyze restricted insertions to fixed alphabet subset
Optimize construction for minimal word length expansion
